weather: Overcast and cool today in Edmonds—high near 57 °F, low around 47 °F. Skies stay mostly cloudy with only a slight (≈15 %) chance of light rain; any showers would be brief. Winds from the south at 5–10 mph add a gentle breeze. Evening turns partly cloudy to mostly clear after midnight, so no umbrella needed for late errands. Dress in layers: the marine air keeps it crisp but not cold.

local_news.findings: Edmonds will begin a major sewer and storm pipe rehabilitation project on May 4, targeting 27 locations with failing infrastructure identified in a 2013 review. The $419,000 project will use cured-in-place pipe (CIPP) technology to repair aging sewer and storm systems throughout the city. Construction crews will work at multiple sites simultaneously, with traffic control measures in place to minimize disruption to residents and businesses.

The South County Fire Board of Commissioners is actively working on redistricting plans to incorporate Edmonds' 42,000+ residents into the Regional Fire Authority's governance structure. Two competing proposals are under consideration: Plan 1 would keep most of Edmonds in a single district, while Plan 2 would split the city approximately at Pine Street. The commission is seeking public input before making a final decision, with implications for how Edmonds residents will be represented on the seven-member fire board.

Edmonds police reported multiple incidents in the past week including a theft arrest of an adult female at Highway 99, a warrant arrest of an adult male, and various assault cases. The police blotter for April 6-12 shows routine but concerning criminal activity in the area, with law enforcement maintaining regular patrols and response protocols.

ai_systems.findings: The autonomous-research stack is hardening into three distinct layers. At the foundation, Google’s new Deep Research Max (Gemini 3.1 Pro) runs 160 parallel web queries overnight, natively charts results, and plugs into private data via the Model Context Protocol—turning due-diligence reports into a morning cron job. One tier above, Google Cloud’s PaperOrchestra orchestrates five specialist agents—outline, plotting, literature, writing, and refinement—to convert raw lab notes and sparse idea summaries into submission-ready LaTeX manuscripts; in human blind tests it beats AI Scientist-v2 by 39-86 % and ties human authors 43 % of the time. Finally, the open-source Mimosa framework and the recursive ROMA meta-agent architecture push beyond fixed pipelines: Mimosa auto-synthesizes task-specific agent graphs and evolves them via execution feedback, hitting 43 % success on ScienceAgentBench, while ROMA uses dependency-aware subtask trees and genetic prompt search to push reasoning depth witho [TRUNCATED]
